meaning: adj.不安定的,易发生变化的
examples: The soil labile carbon in a Pinus taeda plantation in South Jiangsu, China was investigated.
These are more commonly described as the 'labile' metal concentrations.
Special attention should be paid to the storage of heat labile or photosensitive products.
The most labile European country on matters nuclear has, not surprisingly, been Germany, where great engineering and anti-nuclear sentiment have long coexisted uneasily.
Law makers must sometimes find it simply irresistible to craft laws meant to govern our fractious, competitive, emotionally labile, and frankly, mysterious, human behavior.
